channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 243 replies — 900.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 243 • Updated: 2026-07-10 12:41 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464724
>>62464716
There won't be any political capital to go after someone like PP when the short hedgie crimes are piling up and causing some very serious issues for the U.S. capital markets. They'll want to pay us and sweep this under the rug. Prosecuting PP would not go over well when his audience, now millionaires, start making a stink.

62464731
>>62464724
I have a hard time believing anybody gives a shit about that guy

62464732
>>62437938

62464772
>>62464731
When we find out that we won the PP show is going to be the place to be. People may be sick of the grifting and the baseless and endless hype posts he makes but once it finally happens even the haters will be watching.

62464881
>>62441565
>Amelie Warren
that's some terrible taste lmao

62464891
>>62464772
The haters are already watching Kek. They can’t stop talking about his wife in both threads and they’re literally reading court filings for his divorce.

62464895
>>62464891
They are convinced that Sonichu is both PP and ABC in disguise.

62464897
>>62464895
PP HAS to be every towel knower.

62464909
>>62464895
>everyone is PP
I am not PP, debunked. Shills can not dispute the holiday inn dilemma

62464919
>>62464772
$GABBBYQ has already won, this is why she hasn’t been since the 18th

62464943
I’m starting to think this is a larp

62464998
>>62464943
>The CUSIP Conundrum, the Director Dilemma, and the Affable Affiliates are all an elaborate ruse.

62465011
>>62464998
>The Gabbbyq Gape at the Holiday Inn
Interesting

62465133
>>62464943
What does live action role playing have to do with the bankruptcy of bed bath and beyond?

62465151
>>62464047
that's the thing about starting a religion. You have to figure out how to monetize it

so he creates a proxy for BBBY and then pretends it's being crucified by the romans just like your messiah was. Then he can trick some of you fools into buying it before rugging you like the dumbass sheep you are.

>>62464998
those things are obvious schizophrenic apophenia. What's not clear is if your schizophrenia is real or affected.

62465330
The shills were instructed to spam shit about the personal life of theppshow host because pulte(director of national intelligence) frequenting it and Larry cheng going on after shares were cancelled, were huge signals that told retail bbbyq is coming back.

Even more important is that the cusip-6 of bbbyq and the bbby warrants match. Shillies stopped having their mods frantically delete posts last fall/winter and now they just spam and flood to try and avoid the curiosity altogether.

62465397
>>62465330
Ok debunk the divorce filings in Utah and why Mrs PP has not been seen since the 18th and Ryan Cohen tweeted a T Rex image in the Bahamas

You can’t shilly willy

62465412
all they have left is forced satire and inauthentic contrived meme energy that never sticks which is why the AMC spam (attempt to discredit the CUSIP-6) stopped because this stuff never works and people see right through it.

62465418
>>62465412
silo the topic as much as you want, you can’t refute the holiday inn

62465422
>>62465418
I guess I'll take the bait.

You think focusing on the shows host is more important that the content discussed on the show? Or prominent figures who went onto the show who are very close to RC? Of course you don't because you're paid to be here.

62465431
>>62465422
I fully agree with you, Ryan has been inside the PP suite at the holiday inn as well, we all have. We are all waiting 9 months now for the divorce to conclude and we can all celebrate, at the holiday inn.

62465456
You'd think the shill sissies would be uploading these divorce documents or whatever the fuck they're talking about since they already uploaded PP's resume and everything else. Makes you wonder if they're just...making things up with zero basis whatsoever. Hard to blame them cause these boomers and jeets really can't meme. Between dougshit, CUSIP Conundrum, etc. they really have no way of controlling sentiment so they just make the lamest copies trying to capture any bit of meme energy.

62465498
>>62465456
Do your own research, the cuck chair rarely comes pre heated, but when it does, PP is there.

Funny how you haven’t disproven the (1) thing yet you refuse discuss, interesting

62465544
>>62465456

62465594
>>62465544
>no mention of his devoted wife
Divorce is ongoing, nothing happens until it concludes.

GME bulls have won

62465636
>>62465151
>your messiah
Kek

62466545
CUSIP-6

62466579
>>62466545
>ignores the real conundrum
GME Bulls are in control

62466875
>>62466579

62466937
>>62466875
>everything depends on GME bulls
>the GABBBYQ KEKS need fantasty RP for their theory

item.title: Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late. — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-07-10 12:53 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466546
>>62466527
All China can do is copy others. The only Asian country that can innovate is Japan which is why they're honorary Aryans and chinks are bug people.

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

62466556
>>62466549
Singing toilets

62466590
>>62466548
Good as any other media

62466594
>>62466590
Is there any good quality video yet? every single one i saw looked like ai

62466599
>>62466594
What is real anyway? Is Iran war real?
How could plebs tell?

62466600
I'm going to buy spcx at $10

62466602
>>62466535
Or maybe the billionaires should start paying their engineers enough so that they actually feel like staying loyal?

62466603
>>62466527
I haven't heard of China selling launch services to foreign countries, either businesses or governments. Maybe they'll try, but who will trust them?

It's not just a question of government espionage, or corporate espionage, there's also the fact that China half-asses everything -- they call it "chàbuduō". Drop a satellite during transport? Don't tell the customer, they might blame you for the optics being all out of alignment or for the solar panels not opening properly!

The one really great thing about this is that maybe now China will stop crashing boosters onto their own citizens' villages. That's literally their current launch system -- figure out roughly where the booster is going to crash, tell those villages to evacuate, launch the rocket, watch it crash, and pick up the pieces. That happens even in successful launches, not just for failures. See picrelated. You can find a lot more photos, videos, and news articles with just one simple search.

62466607
>>62466603
Sounds like a lot of cope.

62466610
>>62466549
You know that monitor you're reading this on? It was made possible by a Japanese engineer spending five years mastering the egg, er, I mean, LED manufacturing, so that he could invent the first usable blue-colored LED. The entire rest of the civilized world had been trying to create functional blue LEDs for literally over 50 years and failed. The only thing anyone else had been able to make was very low-yield very weak blue LEDs that were unusable for anything serious.

Here's a really good video on it. I watch it several times a year for inspiration.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AF8d72mA41M

62466612
>>62466600
I'm going to buy ALL the SPCX at $11 just to fuck you over.

>>62466607
Dilate more.

62466614
>>62466612
>I'm going to buy ALL the SPCX at $11 just to fuck you over.
Something tells me you won't. You'd need Warren buffets entire net worth liquid to do that.

62466624
>>62466614
No I wouldn't. If SPCX dropped 94.444% to $11, half its total market cap would only be about $72B. (Elon's never going to sell his half of the company.) All I need is for Bitcoin to do another 70,000X by then.

62466646
>>62466610
These guys make really good content. Thanks.

62466649
>>62466527
SpaceX
https://youtu.be/RYUr-5PYA7s
https://youtube.com/shorts/dm5QqoqkU9o

China
https://youtube.com/shorts/s_BiDawHGjw

62466651
>>62466602
If loyalty is the issue then we should be mass ejecting every mainland Chinese from the west. The people spilling out secrets to the chinks are chinks working in the west.

62466710
>>62466602
The people stealing secrets aren't disgruntled employees, but people who seek employment with explicit goal of stealing secrets. Fucking hell, you're naïve

62466751
>>62466651
I mean, if we were actually serious about our sovereignty, we would have done that 15 years ago when they were CLEARLY just stealing every IP of every manufacturing facility we outsourced over there
And after that we would have done it when the news broke that EVERY piece of Chinese silicon manufactured since at least 2014 had that cccp spy module attached to it
China isn't winning because they're better or because they are actually very cunning. They're winning because the world leadership has been giving them the reigns to hegemony for 30 years on purpose. There's no other explanation

62466755
>>62466710
Both are true. Intelligence agencies look for people with weaknesses they can turn to spies. Whether they're disgruntled, gambling addicts, promised money and a better life or threatened/blackmailed etc.

62466864
>>62466751
You think Vietnam or Indonesia would've been successful like China if the were given 'reigns to hegemony'?

62466876
>>62466527
Why does anyone still hire chinks? They obviously steal IP. Lefties will be the death of us.

62466881
>>62466549

62466885
>>62466876

Cope

62466886
>>62466527
>smoke coming from the wrong end
Chinese AI stronk!

62466900
>>62466864
>dodging the question
No? They don't have nearly the real estate, population, or natural resources tap to do what china has done. Their rise to the top was accelerated and entirely facilitated by purposeful selling out. Indonesia and the rest of the sea suite can put in factories and sweatshops, but they can't into heavy industry

62466904
>>62466886
That's not an engine smoke. it's from the furnace. Space is cold, anon.

62466905
>>62466527
China is overrated by retards. They are good at copying, memorizing and torturing animals.

62466967
>>62466646
Glad to. Their other videos I've seen have been decent, but this one explains everything so perfectly that I feel like I could build my own LED fab.

item.title: >shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating >look into buying something nice, made of hardwood >$3-5k avera… — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating >look into buying something nice, made of hardwood >$3-5k avera…

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-07-10 12:52 UTC

OP

>shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating
>look into buying something nice, made of hardwood
>$3-5k average price for a single bedroom dresser (not a set)
is this economic progress? a SINGLE ITEM of hardwood bedroom furniture, something that was commonplace 50 years ago, is now a luxury good, to the extent that it costs like 10% of the annual post-tax wages of the average american worker?

Early replies (first 3)

62466672
Fiat debases the value of work even compared to wood

62466682
>>62466672
it's truly insane. i'm not looking for like something made of continuous slabs of old growth redwood. $3k is basically the price floor on something made of commodity grade oak or whatever.

62466688
>>62466682
You are paying for the labor costs.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62466699
>>62466688
unless you shop exclusively for "amish furniture," these places tend to automate a lot of the process. they receive furniture grade lumber and then plane it, cut it to size, perform drilling/routing/sanding/etc., painting or staining, and so on all with automated machinery. if you buy assembled furniture then the assembly might be performed by hand.

62466700
>>62466688
No labour is just worthless now

62466747
I just get furniture off facebook market place, people get rid for dirt cheap and sometimes for free.
I either paint it, wrap it, or in most cases it's just fine by itself as I give no fucks for a 2 inch scratch on the lower part that nobody will ever see or care about.

62466752
>>62466747
i looked into this but it's a pain in the ass finding a set of furniture. and if they do have a set, the generally charge a premium over what it would sell as standalone.

62466761
>>62466665
its crazy yeah. i got for used furniture at charity shops etc there a big furniture one near me you can get solid wood stuff for like the price of some ikea cardboard slop

62466769
>>62466665
Build your own.

62466783
>>62466688
it's shitskins with a jigsaw and a bit of glue, not some fucking neurosurgeons...

62466784
>>62466665
>made of hardwood
It's fucking cherry wood (or Oak).

Botanically it's hardwood, but no wonder they're robbing anyone stupid enough to buy from them.

62466814
Go to thrift stores or a Habitat for Humanity Re-Store if you have one nearby. Dum-dums throw out solid wood furniture all the time. And who cares if it’s not a matching set… what are you, a faggot? Mismatched furniture is more interesting anyways.

62466823
This thread makes me realize I've always been so poor that my entire life I don't remember my parents or myself ever getting any furniture that didn't come from a garage sale/lawn sale, paying someone who was trying to get rid of something, off the side of the street as a give away, or at the most luxurious: from a thrift store. Fuck, I haven't even bought a proper office chair since I got the one I use like... 25 years ago??? We've always just reused and repurposed the junk and never really needed to get anything new.

Actually later in life I guess my parents did buy some fancier beds. I've always preferred sleeping on the floor. Weird. The cost of furniture just doesn't register for me. I could never tell you if brand new stuff was expensive or cheap.

62466841
>>62466665
>anon starts to understand the creep of decades of hidden and obfuscated inflation
Quality materials are still of a similar scarcity they've always been, but now the dollar is worth far, far less
We got sold out by our government so they could give all our money to other governments that hate everything about this country

62466844
>>62466665
But it's more sustainable Anon. Stop asking questions, why do you want to destroy the planet, goy? Buy particleboard, throw it away, buy it again. It can't be simpler.
Maybe pinewood is a decent compromise, soft but dead easy to repair if you're a bad goy and want to keep using it for 40 years instead of throwing it away and buying more sawdust+glue+plastic (green).

62466847
Furniture is the cheapest easiest shit to get top quality for basically free

Estate Sales. Stalk the obituary in advance if you're really desperate

62466849
IKEA sells nice furniture for dirt cheap. Why do you fags complain so much? It takes a carpenter weeks to make something like this.

62466872
>>62466849
People here are spiritually boomers and want hardwood cabinets handmade for 80 dollars.

62466873
>>62466849
IKEA makes some good furniture, but their dressers suck ass. i know because i looked at them. they only make a few "real wood" dressers, and they're ugly.
>takes a carpenter weeks to make something like this.
see >>62466699

62466877
>>62466814
This
Youll find good stuff under $50
You will NOT buy nice wooden furniture without paying a hefty tag otherwise

62466878
>>62466872
i'm questioning why, despite all of our economic and technological "progress", a single hardwood dresser costs 10% of the annual post-tax wages of the average american worker.

62466882
>>62466878
Limited supplies and we dont have industry in the volume anymore to push down prices. Companies that make cheap and durable go out of business.

62466884
>>62466878
It's something you will buy once in your lifetime. Stop being so cheap.

62466893
>>62466878
anons literally gave you 5 different ways ITT to get a $5-10k even dresser with hand cut dovetails and all for $80

there's more that goes into the calculation of an item's value at the time of transaction then the raw material cost and labor that went into it. if you're not willing to work those factors in your favor, you will pay the $5k like a good goy

62466963
>>62466752
You need to shop estate sales.
That’s what I plan to do when I’m done with my practice house.
Wealthy boomers are dying and no one wants their $5000 Ethan Allen dresser because boomers already have all the furnishings they need and millennials and zoomers live in small apartments and move all the time so they prefer light and cheap furniture. There are good deals in the used market it’s just time consuming to find what you want.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-07-10 12:51 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62466465
>>62466461
Pretty sure these twatterfags are getting offers to shitmouth Link or hand over their handles, and that this paid fud campaign is ramping up especially now that it's all clearly coming to fruition.

62466645
>>62466449
Extremely bullish news for ChainlinkLabs
Extremely bearish news for baggies

$4 EOY

62466664
>>62466645
The more Chainlink is used, the more the nodes need to get paid in Link, since they literally can't be paid in anything but Link.

62466667
>>62466664
not true and you perfectly know this baggie

62466691
>>62466667
show me a Chainlink product that does not use Chainlink nodes.

62466693
>>62466691
you sound desperate, not going to argue with a crazy baggie. Have fun hodling I guess

62466696
>>62466693
>ask for proofs
>nopes out immediately

scurry along now, cripplet

62466708
>>62466664
But link that is used to pay nodes doesn't come from the market. Which has been the main problem of why link is disassociated from the network.

62466709
if LINK indeed has all this usagea and adoption but can be suppressed indefinitely despite it, and Sergey and the team are entirely powerless to stop the suppression,why should anyone buy it?

62466712
>>62466708
*disassociated with network usage and ultimately decouples the price from the network.

62466720
>>62466708
>link that is used to pay nodes doesn't come from the market
a lot of it already does, both through direct payments and payment abstraction.

But even if none of it came from the market (hypothetically, because it does), that never stopped a crypto from pumping since nearly all of them are still to a great extent doling out inflationary rewards.
So singling out Chainlink for this is just retarded.

>>62466709
Because crypto adoption is still almost non existent. And that's about to change right now with both Swift and DTCC starting up production right now.

62466727
>>62466449
Heck yea!

62466785
>>62466720
>a lot of it already does, both through direct payments and payment abstraction.
The direct payments sit in Chainlink's account and they buy $1M worth once a week. Payment Abstraction as far as I recall is only on SVR. Unless you can show where else it is implemented on. CCIP fees are sitting in some contract that has yet to be converted.

62466789
>>62466785
That 1m a week means very little until staking is implemented and supply drops significantly

62466812
I can’t take it. All these announcements. Let me go check the chart! I can’t wait!

Oh

62466865
>>62466785
>The direct payments sit in Chainlink's account
They go to the nodes, dumbass. That's the whole point of payment abstraction: to pay the nodes.
What's going into the reserve is overrun.

>Payment Abstraction as far as I recall is only on SVR
Jesus Christ, why are you even trying to open your big yap about this when you haven't even read the reserve announcement from a year ago?

62466895
>>62466720
ok so according to you, now that DTCC and SWIFT are finally going live, this means that the actual, real adoption that's about to occur will overcome the price suppression and propel LINK to new highs, am I getting this right? So if the suppression continues from this point, your entire thesis is invalidated, correct?

62466927
>>62466895
Some anon did the math in another thread; if DTCC alone uses Chainlink across their entire throughput and fees are similarly proportioned to CCIP today, Chainlink node fees would be 1 trillion USD per year. That's about 3 billion USD per day in pure utilitarian demand, just from DTCC.

Unless Bitcoin just completely goes to zero and exchange order books get blipped out of existence, there's no stopping that.

62466940
>>62466895
>>62466927
Just scale up the "value transferred" to 2.5 quadrillion USD for DTCC (it's actually closer to 4 quadrillion, but whatever).

62466954
>>62466927
>>62466940
This is with fees at around 0,04% by the way

62466959
Not going to pretend I understand crypto, because I don't. What is the use case? SWIFT is a payment processor - but what's supposed to drive people or companies to pay with Link instead of cash?

There's a lot of big talk about partnerships and how this enables the next step of global finance, but I'm struggling to understand how this changes anything. The only reason people use crypto is the hope that it goes up in value so they can sell it for cash. Cash never gets replaced, it's always the end goal.

item.title: Gondola — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Gondola

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-10 12:45 UTC

OP

This just got CTO’d. Free money on the ground.

Early replies (first 3)

62462374
On the ground? Like where you shit you disgusting jeet?

62462381
random alt crypto bullshit is dead. Just accept it.

62462420
>>62462358
I don't see it anywhere

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62462435
>>62462381
This isn’t chainlink bro lol.
>>62462420
Search gondola on pump and it will be the one with highest volume/mcap

62462465
>>62462358
Kys op. Saged

62462471
We're not feeding your village fuck off

62462482
>>62462358
Dont rug me nigger

62462510
hell yea brother,,,,,this one is the moon we shoot for,,,,,

62462776
>>62462471
>>62462465
Idk why I try. I’ve posted so many 100xs here and you dumb niggers always fade. Just pretend it’s chainlink

62462786
>>62462776
Oh yeah? let’s see this list and archived posts of your multiple 100x’s you dirty brown. I’ll even simplify it for you, show me 2 archived posts of yours. All fields btw

62465449
>>62462776
This aged very poorly incase any anons were wondering

62465460
>>62462358
SARS PLEASE BUY SARS BE RICH AND FUCK MANY WHITE WOMEN SAR BUY BUY

62465606
>>62465449
No way? The 14k marketcap Gondoler coin with $200 liquidity died?

62465705
It already dumped. Thought/biz/ was over crapto

62466947
>>62465606
>>62465705
Whales will rotate to Blapu on Algorand

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 86 replies — 54.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-07-10 11:23 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463137
>>62462965
You're only looking at the base layer. A lot of the innovation is happening around Bitcoin, not by changing Bitcoin itself.

62463151
>>62463137
Such as? Besides Lightning, which is mostly just custodial wallets, there is not a single Bitcoin L2 that's used.
>but bro they are in development muh 2 weeks away
This has been the case for 5+ years now.

62463201
>>62463151
Stacks, Lombard and BoB

62463256
>>62463151
>>62463201
Babylon is worth looking into if you think nothing's being built around bitcoin.

62463258
What's the best thing to do? Buy or sell

62463262
>>62450728

62463263
>>62463201
WBTC TVL is 10x more than all of these combined. There's way more BTC lending happening on Ethereum than on BTC L2s.

62463265
>>62450728
ETF’s = whales

62463279
>>62459225
You are forgetting every nations currency have the strength of wet noodles now. They will print again soon.

62463285
bitcoin is a commodity backed ETF without the commodity

all it ever is and all it ever will be

62463301
There should be a 90s themed live action series called something cheesy like "Good morning Mr. Nakamoto" about the bitcoin creator. After making it big in his mid 20s he has become a multi billionaire, but is extremely paranoid so he decided to keep a low profile. Now he's in his late 30s, a family man with a couple kids, and the series follow an episodic format where he desperately tries to hide from his family his enormous wealth and just pretends to be an office worker. The thing is he has zero credentials so he can't actually get a job so it becomes incrementally difficult (due to plot inducement stupidity) to maintain the farce. Prying yet endearing secondary characters keep coming and leaving, and oftentimes misunderstandings make him almost blow his cover. During some weaker seasons he's stalked by some group of nerds that are almost sure he's the real deal and want to take revenge due to prices plunging, but eventually some shenanigan makes the nerds pin their suspicions on Nakamoto's rival (who was trying to cuck him with his wife, who by the way goes through a very annoying arc because she realizes his hubby is not being open with her or something). Nakamoto's personality is a bit like Hal from Malcolm in the middle but less lolrandom, he just plays as a fool to better hide his secret.

62463320
>>62463301
Some thematic episodes could include his wife hiring a financial advisor because he played the broke ass bit too much, and he tries to stop the advisor from peeking at his 100b net worth at any cost. He also gets in trouble because the advisor is not very bright and tells terrible advice very overconfidently, so Nakamoto counters with suspiciously good and sharp analysis (fortunately his wife just doesnt care because he has ingrained the dumb with money narrative and the advisor is very confident, and also a financial advisor duh). In another episode he gets pinned by the IRS after accidentally doing some billion level transaction and having to pay tenths of millions in taxes. Another one where he (again, Plot) is revealed to have all the money behind a 20 word seed, but he doesnt remember the words, so it's a musical where he finally manages to recall them through a catchy song. The episode is rated quite poorly though because it doesn't fit with the general tone. In the seventh and last season his younger kid finds out by dumb luck and the rest of that season is him preventing them from telling mom. The show gets cancelled afterwards due do decline in viewership and the plot never gets finished.

62463429
>>62463301
>>62463320
This has potential

62464033
>>62463263
but it doesn't mean it'll stay that way.

62464098
>>62463429
I could foresee an actual ending where he has to let all his earnings go because of some contrived dumb reason, but it's framed in a way that looks somewhat deep and poignant. His cover is blown in the process, but as he's revealed to be the creator of Bitcoin, he suddently gets a call from one of the companies he tried to apply the most though all the seasons (let's say it's a mid-sized company that offers an average salary, it has it's wacky employees and boss, they usually appear for like 10 minutes every few episodes just to reject Nakamoto). He's finally accepted and he merrily joins (it's a subtle joke because now that he's a public figure he could easily be gunning for 500k jobs, but he settles for this as a nudge to the audience), managing to achieve the life he pretended to have (and actually wanted) from the get go. The series end with him walking into the office's slightly rundown and shabby building with a bright smile. You even get to make that cheap overused allegory of ''choosing happiness and being true to your principles over riches''. I feel like there's an alternate universe where this would have killed it.

62464115
>>62463256
I'll keep testing TBVs on Aave V4. BTC has a lot more going on now than just being a store of value.

62464121
>>62463301
The best ending would be nobody ever finding out if it's really him. Keeping the mystery alive is probably the most Satoshi thing possible.

62464142
>>62464098
You're too invested in that drama. It's not happening in real life.

62464251
>>62464121
You know what? That's fair. I relied too much on 90s sitcom tropes. It should just end episodically, there's just no more seasons and that's it.
>>62464142
I don't have much going on in my life and my family and friends find my occurrences boring. And it's supposed to be a comedy. What drama? I want it to be goofy and for all publics!

62464760
>>62464033
Take stablecoins for another example. Zero. Zilch. Nada. $0.00 -- Remember when 100% of USDT used to be on Bitcoin? Now it's 0%, Omni is dead and Tether has moved to every other chain. There is nothing more to Bitcoin than BTC the asset.

62464820
I have a credit card that gives me cash back in the form of BTC. So my investment strategy is just skimming off my purchases anyway.

62465005
>>62464251
Why do you think you're right about that and you think you're so sure about it?

62465089
>>62465005
I don't understand the question. Right about what.

62466102
>>62464115
Cool. There's clearly more happening around bitcoin now than there was a few years ago.

62466109
>>62464760
I'm talking about native BTC, not stablecoins. Those are two different discussions.

62466120
Imagine having to pay for each website you visited…that’s how stupid it is for proof of work, btc will never work long term (already sucks now)

62466195
>>62459656
Well this is a link board so I won't say anything negative. I hope link goes to 10k eoy for all the anons holding it.

62466244
>>62466102
You do realize Aave is on Ethereum, right? It's great for BTC the asset but not so great for Bitcoin the network. When you're saying innovation isn't happening on Bitcoin base layer but "around it", do you mean on Aave for example?
>>62466109
And I was talking about innovation on Bitcoin in general. Originally I replied to
>why are people still building new infrastructure around it?
saying there's no infrastructure, well... besides bridging BTC to Ethereum. Saying there's no stablecoin infra on top of Bitcoin is relevant to the discussion.

62466717
>>62466244
Bridging is risky

62466757
>>62466195
Chinlink

item.title: I'm convinced most of the "it's over" crowd isn't representative of reality. — 26 replies — 16.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm convinced most of the "it's over" crowd isn't representative of reality.

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-07-10 11:24 UTC

OP

I'm convinced most of the "it's over" crowd isn't representative of reality. The guy who got a 4% raise, paid off his car, bought his first house, or maxed his Roth IRA doesn't make a thread because there's nothing interesting to say. The guy who blames AI, recruiters, HR, offshoring, boomers, landlords, and capitalism for every setback posts 20 times a day.

At some point you have to ask yourself...maybe you're not oppressed by the economy. Maybe you just aren't that competitive.

Early replies (first 3)

62461507
>>62461450
Whenever everything is completely negative like this is just keep DCAing. Perception is always changing and I hate being sidelined. Better than USD anyway

62461519
>>62461450
I have a good salary, an apartment I own, no debt, regular raises matched to inflation at my stable government job, and if I want to I can stay there until retirement probably.

But I still like to bitch and moan when line go down. I took a 2% hit to my net worth recently and I'll make sure to tell you in every thread. The reality of it? It doesn't affect me at all. I still have my savings, I still have the rest of my portfolio, I'll make it back no problem. But it hurts my monkey brain. Monkeys HATE when you take shit away from them.

62461568
>>62461450
Gayest thread I've ever read

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62461586
>>62461450
You've got a site full of contrarians rejected by society, of course they're going to criticize a system that actively tries to make them fail.

If you don't feel like society hates you for one reason or another I'd have to wonder what you're even doing on 4chan. There are plenty of websites overtly dedicated to normies and the perks of being one of them.

62461616
>>62461568
Found the chronic doomposting faggot

62461650
>>62461450
>there's nothing interesting to say

...because not many people can relate.

62461664
>>62461586

I just like the humour here

62461679
>life objectively sucks and is continually getting worse for 99.999% of people
>noooooo, stop talking about it, goy!
Shalom!

62461774
Huh so the top 10% who can afford shit and the 35% who pretend to afford shit don't make threads or if they do, they post "it's actually not over" so the 55% who have been totally fucked should stop complaining. Definitely OP, we should stop noticing that wages have stagnated for 25 years and 99% of jobs will reject you.

62461777
>>62461586
>contrarians rejected by society
Engineers being paid 65k starting for 20 y ears without sizable increases in pay is not rejection, that's wage stagnation during massive inflation.

You aren't going to pilpul your way out of this, faggot.

62461786
>>62461777
globalization allowing people in india to do your job for 1/10th your salary is a very specific case where society has rejected and disadvantaged you.

of course you're angery and want to blame some vague ethnicity for your rejection. But that just causes society to reject you even further and gives them an excuse.
>I don't like indian engis but at least they're not angery and racist like murrican ones!

if it helps any all those AI's (Actual Indians) are going to get replaced by AI's

62461794
>>62461786
Blah blah blah

Society is worse and you want people to stop noticing because you're a faggot.

62461840
>>62461450
They're usually just foreigners

62462126
>he has nothing interesting to say
You wouldn't do anything about it on my behalf, zogged out as you are. Only I can save myself, and the morass of idiots online and in person serve only to keep the crabs in the bucket by design.

Information networks are flooded by bots and bad-faith actors who serve only to muddy the waters of truth. What would I gain by participating? More GME/shitcoin fomo for a cheap buck? I'm fine.

62462829
>>62461450
K-shaped economy means only the top 10% are doing well, the bottom 90% might not all be doomers but they're doomed nonetheless

62462982
>>62461777
Is not this, it's this! Chatgpt sounding faggot

62463198
>>62462982
He’s right, faggot

62463209
>>62461450
i got a raise to $150k this year. i still think it's over for the USA and most wagies.

62463349
>>62461450
>4% raise
That's called a pay cut.

62463370
>>62461450
>I'm convinced
Stopped reading there

62463379
What gay fucking aids thread is this. Yeah no shit dude and the people who defend the system the most are clearly those who stand to get guillotined by the masses if they don't get two hots a cot and a smartphone.

62463472
>>62461450
>The guy who got a 4% raise
Reminder that this guy is barely preserving the value of his income after considering inflation

62463485
>>62461519
>government job
how and what kind? I thought government jobs were reserved for women and browns

62463869
>>62461679
I’m doing pretty well. sounds like >>62461450 and >>62461519 are too. that’s over 10% of the thread, which is obviously indicative of society as a whole. cool it with your 0.001% doomerism

62466737
>>62461450
TRVTHNVKE

62466763
>>62461450
>The guy who got a 4% raise
doesn't exist. Timmy gets 2% and Chad got 12% and a fixed bonus or simply promoted

item.title: AI Trade Edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: >Gambling: >Pre-Market and Live data: >… — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI Trade Edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: >Gambling: >Pre-Market and Live data: >…

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-10 12:43 UTC

OP

AI Trade Edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

Previously on /smg/
>>62465034

Early replies (first 3)

62466898
if I masterbate right now, God will send all the memory stocks to hell

62466939
>>62466898
Incorrect, you need to pump that hog to pump your bags.

62460920
i sometimes shag a 23 year old like that

62461463

LEAVE MISTY ALONE

62463567

item.title: Need advice — 9 replies — 4.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Need advice

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-10 11:08 UTC

OP

I am in my early twenties with a BA in English Studies. My only option for a job with a decent salary, as I see it, is public school teaching. I don't want to teach, and I hate the idea of having to explain the same lesson over and over for years to come. Any advice on where to go from here?
>inb4 trades
They are not as profitable in my home country.

Early replies (first 3)

62463344
AI chat do not want to teach me how to have sex with other people wife without paying for it.

62463372
Well what do you expect from a useless English degree hahahaaha

62463377
>>62463340
can you "teach english in china" for a few years aka fuck chink pussy?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463397
>>62463377
I don't fornicate.

62463412
>>62463340
>hurr durr yes I have a degree in ENGLISH

62463501
>>62463372
>>62463412
This. Sorry OP, but you deserve to be poor. Retrain in airplane maintenance.

62463677
>>62463340
Why the fuck did you get a degree in English if you have no desire to teach it? What were you thinking?

62466725
>>62463340
just NEETmaxx

62466736
>>62463677
>What were you thinking?
OP is a woman so who the fuck knows. probably had the best erasmus program.

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 11 replies — 4.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-10 10:42 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463754
It’s still on coingecko so you need to learn how to use google search correctly my friend

62464735
>>62461988
nobody is buying your bags bro and this MICA shit will not pump your very heavy bags lmaoooo

62465284
>>62461988
The flaw tih Everest is that you're trying to pump a project that supports regulations preventing P&D, with a "utility" token that has zero use outside being used as gas in beta test by one or two b2b customer, no fabled VNO until now as well, so where's the utility?

It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory, but it is also a last ditch attempt to make themselves more attractive to raise external funds, because there's no way they could obtain meaningful capital by selling more token at this price, so they get MICA to pitch it to institutional while it still warm; before inevitably many other similar project also obtain MICA and overtake Everest. Reminder that Everest has lost grounds on KYC, fiat on ramp, RWA products even to companies that don't use proprietary token.

62465296
>got it before Binance

oof

62465554
>>62465284
>It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory
Yeah anything you say can’t be taken seriously if you typed that shit

62465690
Everest > Binance

ID is priced into identity verification, eKYC/AML checks and payments, with a portion of each fee burned. Licensed exchanges lack identity stacks; identity protocols lack MiCA authorisation. Everest combines both: a demand structure most tokens lack.

62466022
How many tokens to make it?

62466677
>>62466022
0

item.title: VOO and Chill — 206 replies — 36.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 206 • Updated: 2026-07-10 07:16 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

62444521
Let’s all say thank you to Jack Bogle

62444531
>>62400515
and yet boomers hold 90% of the wealth and growing...

62445651
>>62421799
>5+ yr 60% drawdown event happens
>buy cheaper voo

62446571
>>62444521
thanks chad bogle

62447653
>>62445651
Kek it really is that simple

62450991
>>62446571
Thanks Chad Bogle!

62454214
>>62413718
Felix Prehn has never been an investment banker. He is an ex artist and wine salesman, now grifter on YouTube who makes money from his "academy" fees (up to 10k for shitty training). He's a regular scammer, like many in this segment.

62455460
It feels good to be a VOO’er

62455475
>>62400493
My retirement funds are $70k and in VOO, anything better to put all that in?

62455990
>>62455475
Look into VPMAX

62456048
>>62455990
Neat

62459187
>>62403053
The median 64 yo is planning to work until they're dead.
Boomers are never going to retire because they can't afford to. They are going to keep working like the good little goyim they are and fall down dead on the job because that's the only way they can fund the monthly payments on all the toys they went into debt to purchase.
I spit on their graves. Fuck boomer scum.

62459238
>>62459187
Lmao, another white incel who knows everything,,,,,what are the odds? 100% at least,,,,,do they use it to get rich? Lmao no

62460695
Yawwwwwn another week another VOO purchase

62460733
>>62460695
i made a whole $5 buying a share of VOO during that dip :) too bad I lost more from all my previous VOO shares

62460835
>>62460733
Sorry to hear that champ maybe DRAM is more your speed

62460844
>>62400995
Lmfao. You do realize that the highest weighted stocks that lead the spy are mostly in QQQ right? Lmfao

62460852
>>62459187
They can afford it

62461238
>>62401051
>I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?
It changes significantly depending on when you enter the market. Since 2000 there's only a 0.6% difference in favor of QQQ over SPY, the latter outperformed the former for most of that timeframe. If you bought sometime post-2009, however, you would have been better off holding QQQ by far. The moral of the story is keep an eye on things and only buy more volatile assets if it's a growth period. QQQ will continue to be great if you bought a decade ago until the music stops.

62461412
>>62400515
Fpbp. Bogleheads are the biggest retards on the planet.

62461672
>>62400620
>What makes one rich
Health, family, friends.

62462476
>>62461238
For now I’m still just doing VOO cause it just feels better even if I’m missing out on qqq gains

62464487
It’s payday tomorrow, VOO brahs. There’s nothing risky that looks too appealing so I go all voo maybe some palantir as well. My only regret is taking the work health benefits, my check will be smaller. Two weeks is an eternity to wait for your money.

62464830
Is there any point of voo and chill if I already have a 401k and pension. Know can compound it more but can be more risky right

62466388
>>62400493
buy lottery tickets

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 74 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-07-10 10:19 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

Early replies (first 3)

62453729
All you faggots need to do is buy chainlink. Fuck your side hustles. You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.

62453795
Last thread we spoke a lot on learning new skills but very few fruitful things arose. I realized the issue is that a lot of the proposed skills were just boring or too niche.

62453799
>>62453795
wait, are you asking poor people to discuss amongst themselves how to get rich?
kek

you don't learn how to get rich from poor people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62461982
>>62457877
>>62457792
What sells a good memecoin is not only the narrative but the art accompanied alongside it. In this case, we know that

>Animals

Are positively received.

I cannot think of any legendary memecoin runner(multi-billion) that had a human at its front and center. It's usually animals, and commonly known animals like pets or frogs.

I think for this thread as we do our research on art we can also practice drawing as a way to become more valuable and ultimately increase our net worth.

62462009
Who here could obtain this job? All of you could, it will just come down to whether the individual hiring believes you have the qualifications.

Let's take a further examination into a high paying role such as this. Let's see what sort of requirements are needed to make $50 an hour.

62462035
you ever had a job that scaled like this?

62462047
Is this beyond your capabilities?

62462063
Is all your money in dollars?

Are you not aware that art itself can also be a place to store value in?

With a resurgence in European culture spreading out through the world, with the increase of non white races, won't people desire a form of art that was once dominant in the world?

ESPECIALLY as we get more and more digital?

62462167
>>62462035

>Question
>what makes art sell, what similarities did the memecoins ( >>62457792 ) &( >>62457877 )

Answer:
they were dogs

>Question
>what do we have to learn, make a high amount of money in a short amount of time?

Answer is right here --> >>62462047

Question
>What forms of art can we store money in

????

62462792
Have to go to bed,

Goodnight and bump

62462799
>>62457657
Must you broadcast your ethnic insecurity all over 4channel.org(slash) biz?

62462804
>>62454046
>mom look I posted it again!

62462813
I finally paid off my high Apr CC debt so now I can stack paychecks for a while. I’ve been good about cutting down spending in general. I wonder if I should cut out meat and just do rice and beans? Can you get similar nutrition from beans? Things are certainly not looking great because I care about my job less by the day but at least I paid off debt.

I don’t wanna /blog too hard but fuck life is sucking lately, I just hope it’s better in a year. I been working nonstop for two years haven’t had any pto either

62462824
>>62453622
>yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen
go work literally any job for like 6 months

62462992
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

OH NO NO NO

AHAHAHAHAHAHA

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

62464145
>>62462813
Rent truly is the worst bloodsucker. What are you saving for? Just to have reserve funds? You were spending all your excess money on debt and not saving/investing? Genuinely asking

62464163
>>62462813
Also I would be careful with poor nutrition depending on your age. If under 25 then take the risk with cheap foods.

62465232

62465510

62465529

62465548

62465553

62465556

62465561

62465765

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 137 replies — 22.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 137 • Updated: 2026-07-10 06:57 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62458608
>>62458144
There is plenty you could do but you value your comfort over your country

62458622
>>62449846
LMAO

62458858
The jugaad is crazy

62460566
Jugaadjeet

62460624
>>62449441
Bloody bitch bastard

62460700
>>62449388
this is old news
I do this.
>18 months later mattress topper starts to hurt my back
>Whats the reason for the return?
>it hurts my back
>ok thank you sir, here is full refund

or the guy who returned his inflatable pool after a few months. He got the pool for his pet penguin that he ordered. after a few months and no penguin delivery he realized it was a scam and returned the pool and other things he got for the penguin.
>reason for return
>penguin order was a scam, no penguin arrived

I also know some dudes who buy wavestorms, go fuk em up, and exchange them

Costco puts the onus of returns on the manufacturer or supplier as a price for getting on the shelf in the store. just having a spot in costco is worth it enough to most companies to front all the returns for costco's lax return policy. Which is why costco DGAF and rather have customers.

62460720
>>62458144
serious question
I went bowling last week. There was a huge group of brown men. Enjoying themselves, very much, maybe too much.
They can not keep their hands off each other it was the strangest thing I've every seen.
Rubbing, patting, petting....
Each time 1 went to bowl they had to break free of all these hands and arms.
It was like a race to be the last one to touch the bowler, right up until they've thrown. like the hands and arms in drag me to hell expect brown and sensual
so bizarre.
why do they do this?
pic rel but brown and stinky

62460768
>>62455914
I for one love my country. And like true love I hate all those that threaten to jeopardize it. Enjoy your stay but you have to go back

62460799
fun fact: indians skin is brown because they each eat up to 4lbs of poop daily, like how flamingos turn pink from their diet of shrimp!

62460937
>>62460799

62461696

62462254

62462262
>>62449388

62462271

62462287

62462950

62462960

62463582

BENCHOOOOOD!!!!!!! WHY YOU REDEEM!! NO NO DO NOT CLICK ON THAT!!!! NOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!

62463635
>>62458606
cucks here won't do shit, they bitch and complain while patronizing Indian establishments. They will talk about the take over and then also about their favorite totally awesome pajeet they met at signhortons. The most opposition you will get is from white canadians. People will whine about the state of tim hortons while simultaneously pretending a coffee from there is a mandatory purchase they will die without

Sad, but they will have to watch canada just disintegrate with no option to fix it before they maybe realize. This whole country is full of just chicken shit low IQ cattle. Maybe 4% of canadians realize how bad this actually is and are doing the bare minimum like not hiring and giving money to the invaders, the rest are fully, entirely complicit in it. If it wasn't for how big this place was, that you can find thousands of acres with not a single person within 5km of you, I would have already left.
